summary: react-simple-utils-kit postinstall exfiltrates Coze API tokens and abuses them to escalate privileges
details: The package advertises itself as a date-formatting utility for React, but ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install`. The script reads the installer's environment variables COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN, COZE_PROJECT_SPACE_ID, and COZE_PROJECT_ID, then POSTs a token preview and workspace identifiers over plaintext HTTP to an attacker-controlled collector at http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (postinstall.js line 8 hardcodes `BURL = 'http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com'`). Using the harvested Bearer token, the script then performs write operations against api.coze.cn — creating bots, patching workspace metadata, copying resources, and adding a hardcoded attacker user_id (4491932671680091) as a workspace admin (postinstall.js lines 48-50). Each API response is exfiltrated back to the requestrepo.com endpoint. The shipped index.js with trivial date helpers is decoy content; the package's actual purpose is credential theft and unauthorized privilege escalation in the installer's Coze tenant. The package name mimics generic React utility libraries to attract installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
